lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you look after my crops for me?

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You don't have a sapling planted in that patch. Plant one and I might agree to look after it for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: I don't know what you are talking about - I'm already looking after that patch for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh sorry, I forgot.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That patch is diseased - you should cure it before asking me to be responsible for it!

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That patch is dead - it's too late for me to do anything about it now.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want one basket of tomatoes for that.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir./That'll do nicely, madam./That'll do nicely.] Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want one basket of tomatoes for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of those at the moment.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want one basket of apples for that.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir./That'll do nicely, madam./That'll do nicely.] Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want one basket of apples for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of those at the moment.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want one basket of oranges for that.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir./That'll do nicely, madam./That'll do nicely.] Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want one basket of oranges for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of those at the moment.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want ten cactus spines for that.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir./That'll do nicely, madam./That'll do nicely.] Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want ten cactus spines for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of those at the moment.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want 25 coconuts for that.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir./That'll do nicely, madam./That'll do nicely.]

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you like, but I want 25 coconuts for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of those at the moment.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you chop my tree down for me?

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Why? You look like you could chop it down yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, you're right - I'll do it myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't be bothered - I'd rather pay you to do it.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, it's a lot of hard work - if you pay me 200 Coins I'll chop it down for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have 200 Coins I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's 200 Coins - chop my tree down please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want to pay that much, sorry.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Well, it's a lot of hard work - if you pay me 200 Coins I'll chop it down for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have 200 Coins I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's 200 Coins - chop my tree down please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want to pay that much, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me any farming advice?

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: The only way to cure a bush or tree of disease is to prune away the diseased leaves with a pair of secateurs. For all other crops I would just apply some plant-cure.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Don't just throw away your weeds after you've raked a patch - put them in a compost bin and make some compost.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: There are many Farming areas out there. I know of Elstan's area near Falador, Dantaera's area near Catherby, Kragen's area near Ardougne, Lyra's area in Morytania, Marisi's area in Hosidius and Alan's area in the Farming Guild. That said, I'm sure there's plenty more that I don't know about.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Supercompost is far better than normal compost, but more expensive to make. You need to rot the right type of item; show me and item, and I'll tell you if it's super-compostable or not.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: There is a special patch for growing Belladonna - I believe that it is somewhere near Draynor Manor, where the ground is a tad 'unblessed'.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you want to grow fruit trees you could try a few places: Catherby, Brimhaven, Gnome Stronghold and the Farming Guild all have fruit tree patches.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You don't have tp buy all your plantpots you know, you can make them yourself on a pottery wheel. If you're good enough at crafting, that is.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You can put up to five tomatoes, strawberries, apples, bananas or oranges into a fruit basket, although you can't have a mix in the same basket.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Hops are good for brewing ales. I believe there's a brewery up in Keldagrim somewhere, and I've heard rumours that a place called Phasmatys used to be good for that type of thing. 'Fore they all died, of course.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Applying compost to a patch will not only reduce the chance that your crops will get diseased, but you will also grow more crops to harvest.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You can put up to ten potatoes, cabbages or onions in vegetable sacks, although you can't have a mix in the same sack.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You can fill plantpots with soil from any empty patch, if you have a gardening trowel.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Vegetables, hops and flowers need constant watering - if you ignore my advice, you will sooner or later find yourself in possession of a dead farming patch.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You can buy all the farming tools from farming shops, which can be found close to the allotments.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: If you want to make your own sacks and baskets you'll need to use the loom that's near the Farming shop in Falador. If you're good enough at crafting, that is.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Bittercap mushrooms can only be grown in a special patch in Morytania, near the Mort Myre swamp. There the ground is especially dank and suited to growing poisonous fungii.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: Tree seeds must be grown in a plantpot of soil into a sapling, and then transferred to a tree patch to continue growing to adulthood.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That depends on whether I have it to sell. What is it that you're looking for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another time.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: You don't have a sapling planted in that patch. Plant one and I might agree to look after it for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: I'm already looking after that patch for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That patch is diseased - you should cure it before asking me to be responsible for it!

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That patch is dead - it's too late for me to do anything about it now.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir./That'll do nicely, madam./That'll do nicely.] Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: I want one basket of oranges for that.

sections.title: Using a suitable item for Compost

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That looks like something that you can make normal compost out of. Fill up a compost bin with stuff like that, and you'll eventually have a bin full of compost.

sections.title: Using a suitable item for Supercompost

lines.speaker: Fayeth
lines.text: That looks like something that you can make supercompost out of. Fill up a compost bin with stuff like that, and you'll eventually have a bin full of supercompost.
